ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* ISO country code */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the country */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the country */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* Official language of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country ID */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the population data */,
  "population_growth" REAL /* Population growth metric for the year */,
  "total_population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country for the year */,
  "urban_population" INTEGER /* Urban population of the country for the year */,
  "rural_population" INTEGER /* Rural population of the country for the year */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the country for the year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "geographic_regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the geographic region */,
  "region_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_region_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country ID */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region ID */,
  "sub_region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sub-region ID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_region_mapping_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES geographic_regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_region_mapping_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user ID */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country ID */,
  "data_access_level" TEXT /* Level of data access for the user (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the data source */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data (e.g., CSV, JSON, XML) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_imports" (
  "import_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data import */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source ID */,
  "import_date" TEXT /* Date of data import */,
  "import_status" TEXT /* Status of data import (e.g., success, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("import_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_imports_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)
